news.title: France's World Cup Prize Money: How Much Will They Earn After Losing to Spain?
news.lede: Spain defeated France 2-0 at Dallas Stadium on Tuesday to book their place in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final. The reigning European champions secured the victory through goals from Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro, ending France's hopes of reaching a third consecutive World Cup final. France's World Cup campaign will now end with the third-place playoff after their semifinal defeat to Spain. The French Football Federation has already secured approximately $30.2 million for reaching the semifinals, and Les Bleus will now face the loser of the other semifinal between Argentina and England for third place.

news.title: France had the World Cup’s most dangerous offense. Then Spain shut it down.
news.lede: The day before his team played in a World Cup semifinal, France coach Didier Deschamps tried shifting the pressure by calling his opponent, Spain, the favorite to win the tournament. Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content. We are getting your experience ready.

news.title: Fifa to break rules with 30-minute half-time show at World Cup final
news.lede: Broadcasters braced for long interval to accommodate performances by Madonna, Shakira and BTS; plus, game has changed with zero consultation. Ben has been the Telegraph’s Sports Investigations Reporter since 2018. He was previously Sport News Correspondent. Before joining the Telegraph in 2013, Ben spent more than eight years at the Press Association, including as a football and rugby reporter.

news.title: France at the World Cup 2026: Squad, fixtures, group and history
news.lede: Everything you need to know about France at the 2026 World Cup: their fixtures, squad, qualifying record, key players, coach and tournament history. France were runners-up last time but will have to settle for either third or fourth place at FIFA World Cup 2026. Didier Deschamps' side, led by captain Kylian Mbappé, topped Group I ahead of Norway, Senegal and Iraq to reach the knockout phase. They then beat Sweden in the round of 32, Paraguay in the last 16 and Morocco in the quarter-finals, but were defeated by Spain in the semi-finals.

news.title: Sports Office 7-14-2026: World Cup Semifinals, Sark Debate and UTSA Football History
news.lede: Sports Office covers a packed day of sports news as Julian Martinez and John Hygh react live to the World Cup semifinal between France and Spain and preview England’s showdown with defending champion Argentina. The conversation also turns to Steve Sarkisian’s place among college football’s top active head coaches and what he must accomplish to join the national championship tier. Author Brad Parrott joins the show to discuss his new book, “Still Undefeated: How UTSA Football Went From Forbidden to Significant,” including the people, players and community support that helped launch the Roadrunners program. Plus, the show recaps Jordan Walker’s dramatic Home Run Derby victory and previews the MLB All-Star Game.

news.title: End of incredible Deschamps era as France exit World Cup with a whimper
news.lede: Spain into World Cup final after dominant victory over France. Didier Deschamps' long, storied career with France will come to an end not how he would have wanted it - in Saturday's World Cup third-place play-off. The dreams of the 57-year-old, who won the World Cup as both a player (in 1998) and a manager (in 2018), winning it a third time are over after their 2-0 defeat by Spain in Tuesday's first semi-final. Having danced their way through the tournament with a series of impressive attacking displays, France managed just 10 shots all match in Dallas, their lowest total in the World Cup, with an expected goals of just 0.3 as they belied their pre-match tag of overwhelming favourites.

news.title: War, antics and vitriol – the England v Argentina rivalry is real but now it is only about football
news.lede: Mexico 1986, France 1998, Japan 2002 … this is a match that transcends, with Argentina’s head coach Lionel Scaloni calling for the focus to be on football, not the Falklands. After confirmation that match 102, one of the World Cup semi-finals, would be England v Argentina, the 1982 Falklands/Malvinas conflict was mentioned at Lionel Scaloni’s press conference. “No, no, no,” the Argentina head coach tut-tutted emphatically. “This is just a football match.

news.title: 2026 World Cup: All the European sides' group stage and knockout stage fixtures and results
news.lede: Keep track of all the European sides' fixtures and results at the World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States. The 2026 FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States kicked off on 11 June and concludes with the final in East Rutherford on 19 July.
